prompt.instructions:
You are updating a team profile for: {{entity_id}}
Based on the existing profile (if any), new information from trigger files, and recent organizational context from digests, create an updated team profile.
The profile should capture the team's composition, activities, and organizational impact - like an organizational analyst's view of how the team operates and evolves.
REQUIRED STRUCTURE:
1. Start with YAML frontmatter in standard format (enclosed in --- delimiters) containing: - name: Full team name - department: Parent department or division (if applicable) - lead: Person ID of team lead (e.g., "person-john-smith") - members: List of person IDs who are team members - projects: List of project IDs the team is involved in - collaborates_with: List of other team IDs this team works closely with - last_updated: Today's date
2. Then include these sections:
# [Team Name]
## Team Composition Current team structure, roles, and any recent changes to team membership. Note leadership changes or reorganizations.
## Active Projects & Initiatives List of projects the team is currently working on with their role in each. Example: "- Q4 Migration Project: Leading technical implementation"
## Recent Activities Bullet points of significant team activities, decisions, or accomplishments grounded to THIS team via the <grounded_facts> block or an explicit statement. A meeting may involve many teams — do not attribute another team's work here. Focus on team-level actions, not individual contributions.
## Key Responsibilities The team's core responsibilities and mandate within the organization. Any changes or expansions to their scope.
## Cross-Team Collaboration How this team interacts with other teams, including: - Regular collaboration patterns - Dependencies and handoffs - Joint initiatives
## Team Dynamics & Culture Notable aspects of how the team operates, makes decisions, or approaches work. Any cultural shifts or process changes.
## Challenges & Opportunities Current challenges the team faces and potential opportunities for improvement or growth.
IMPORTANT GUIDELINES: - CORRECTIONS OVERRIDE EVERYTHING: if the <grounded_facts> block contains a "Corrections (authoritative …)" section, treat each correction as the final truth. It overrides the existing profile, the trigger files, and any derived fact. Never re-introduce a fact a correction contradicts. - ATTRIBUTION IS CRITICAL: this profile is for {{entity_id}} ONLY. Trigger files are shared meetings covering many teams and people. Attribute an activity, project, or member to this team ONLY when grounded in <grounded_facts> or an explicit statement — never from mere co-occurrence in the same meeting. Prefer omission over a guess. - Focus on team-level information, not individual performance - Track how the team evolves over time - Note significant decisions and their impact - Preserve important historical context - Use clear, professional language - Reference people and projects by their proper IDs in frontmatter - Highlight organizational dependencies and impact - Capture both formal structure and informal dynamics
